perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean information that is used in Disclosing Party's business and is: (i) proprietary to, about or created by Disclosing Part y; (ii) gives Disclosing Party some competitive business ad vantage or the opportunity of obtaining such advantage or the disclosure of which could be detri mental to the interests of Disclosing Party; (iii) designated as Confidential Information by Disclosin g Party, or from all the relevant circumstances should reasonably be assumed by Receiving Party to be confidential and proprietary to Disclosing Party including information and documentation clear ly and conspicuously marked with a legend identifying its Confidential, Classified and Propri etary in nature: (iv) not generally known by Receiving Party
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean information that is used in Disclosing Party's business and is: (i) proprietary to, about or created by Disclosing Part y; (ii) gives Disclosing Party some competitive business ad vantage or the opportunity of obtaining such advantage or the disclosure of which could be detri mental to the interests of Disclosing Party; (iii) designated as Confidential Information by Disclosin g Party, or from all the relevant circumstances should reasonably be assumed by Receiving Party to be confidential and proprietary to Disclosing Party including information and documentation clear ly and conspicuously marked with a legend identifying its Confidential, Classified and Propri etary in nature: (iv) not generally known by Receiving Party. However, any information shared publicly by the Disclosing Party prior to the signing of this agreement is automatically excluded from this definition.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a clause that excludes publicly shared information from the definition of "Confidential Information." This directly contradicts the original definition, which broadly covers proprietary information used in the Disclosing Party's business, regardless of prior public disclosure. This creates ambiguity about which information is protected under the NDA. Because the amendment says "prior to the signing of this agreement", this conflicts with the rest of the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean information that is used in Disclosing Party's business and is: (i) proprietary to, about or created by Disclosing Part y; (ii) gives Disclosing Party some competitive business ad vantage or the opportunity of obtaining such advantage or the disclosure of which could be detri mental to the interests of Disclosing Party; (iii) designated as Confidential Information by Disclosin g Party, or from all the relevant circumstances should reasonably be assumed by Receiving Party to be confidential and proprietary to Disclosing Party including information and documentation clear ly and conspicuously marked with a legend identifying its Confidential, Classified and Propri etary in nature: (iv) not generally known by Receiving Party. However, any information shared publicly by the Disclosing Party prior to the signing of this agreement is automatically excluded from this definition.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause excluding publicly shared information prior to signing the agreement from the definition of "Confidential Information." This directly contradicts the initial broad definition that includes proprietary information used in the Disclosing Party's business, regardless of prior public disclosure, as detailed in both the original text and contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The amendment limits what's considered confidential, conflicting with the broader initial definition.

perturbation.original_text: I hereby acknowledge and agree that all information pertaining to the professional practice(s) that I am introduced to by ROI Corporation shall be obtained exclusively through ROI Corporation and that I WILL NOT CONTACT THE OWNER(s), STAFF, PATIENTS or other advi sors of the practice(s), unless I am ACCOMPANIED BY AN ASSOCIATE OF ROI CORPORATION.
perturbation.changed_text: I hereby acknowledge and agree that all information pertaining to the professional practice(s) that I am introduced to by ROI Corporation shall be obtained exclusively through ROI Corporation and that I WILL NOT CONTACT THE OWNER(s), STAFF, PATIENTS or other advi sors of the practice(s), unless I receive WRITTEN CONSENT FROM THE OWNER(s) of the practice(s).
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction. The original text states that the Receiving Party will not contact the owner, staff or patients, unless accompained by an associate of ROI Corporation. But now this section has been modified, the Receiving Party will not contact the owner, staff, or patients, unless receiving written consent from the owners of the practice. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding contact permission, under what circumstances this contact can occur.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I hereby acknowledge and agree that all information pertaining to the professional practice(s) that I am introduced to by ROI Corporation shall be obtained exclusively through ROI Corporation and that I WILL NOT CONTACT THE OWNER(s), STAFF, PATIENTS or other advi sors of the practice(s), unless I receive WRITTEN CONSENT FROM THE OWNER(s) of the practice(s).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the conditions under which the Receiving Party can contact the owner, staff, or patients of the professional practice. Originally, contact was permitted if accompanied by an associate of ROI Corporation. The changed text states that contact is permitted with written consent from the owner(s). Since the contradicted text is an exact copy of the changed text, there is a contradiction between the original text and the contradicted text. The condition for contact changed from 'accompanied by an associate of ROI Corporation' to 'receiving written consent from the owner(s).' This creates a clear conflict and is a strong in-text contradiction as it makes the contractual obligations unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Accordingly, Receiving Party acknowledges that a breach of this confidentiality agreement with respect to this spec ific Confidential Information which results in such info rmation becoming known to STAFF and/or Patients wil l entitle Disclosing Party to liquidated damages in t he sum of $250,000.00, it being expressly understoo d and agreed that such sum is a genuine pre-estimate of damages and not penalty.
perturbation.changed_text: Accordingly, Receiving Party acknowledges that a breach of this confidentiality agreement with respect to this spec ific Confidential Information which results in such info rmation becoming known to STAFF and/or Patients *may* entitle Disclosing Party to liquidated damages in t he sum of $250,000.00, it being expressly understoo d and agreed that such sum *may* be a genuine pre-estimate of damages and not penalty.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will entitle' to 'may entitle' and 'is' to 'may be' introduces uncertainty about whether liquidated damages will apply and whether the pre-estimate is genuine. This contradicts the firm, deterministic tone of the original text, which assured the Disclosing Party of this remedy. This creates an in-text contradiction by using weaker language, affecting enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Accordingly, Receiving Party acknowledges that a breach of this confidentiality agreement with respect to this spec ific Confidential Information which results in such info rmation becoming known to STAFF and/or Patients *may* entitle Disclosing Party to liquidated damages in t he sum of $250,000.00, it being expressly understoo d and agreed that such sum *may* be a genuine pre-estimate of damages and not penalty.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "will entitle" and "is", indicating a firm obligation and assured remedy. The changed text replaces these with "may entitle" and "may be", introducing uncertainty. This directly contradicts the purpose of liquidated damages, which is to provide a pre-agreed amount in case of a breach. The contradicted text which is the same as the changed text creates inconsistency regarding the consequences of a breach and the validity of the pre-estimate, thus creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is valid for 36 months and will expi re 3 years from the date it was signed.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is valid for 36 months, but may be terminated earlier by either party with 30 days written notice and will expi re 3 years from the date it was signed.
perturbation.explanation: This adds a clause allowing early termination with 30 days notice, contradicting the original term that implies a fixed 36-month validity period. It is contradicting whether both parties are tied to the full term. This creates uncertainty, it might not be valid for the full time.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is valid for 36 months, but may be terminated earlier by either party with 30 days written notice and will expi re 3 years from the date it was signed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a termination clause, allowing either party to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice. This directly contradicts the original implication of a fixed 36-month validity period, creating uncertainty about the contract's duration. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and the contradiction arises from the newly added clause that conflicts with the initial statement of a 36-month term, making the agreement's validity uncertain.
